data.title: Court documents reveal the breakdown of SBF's seized assets, including approximately $1 billion in financial assets and two private jets
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News Before SBF was tried and jailed for fraud, a federal court outlined the size of his assets, and the government subsequently seized about $1 billion in financial assets and two aircraft.
On Tuesday, the final confiscation order issued by the U.S. District Court for the Southern District of New York officially cut off SBF's ownership in a dozens-page property list. Court documents disclosed a large number of assets owned by him, including a large amount of cryptocurrency from Alameda Research, a trading company co-founded by SBF.
The documents show that Alameda's assets in Binance include $56 million in XRP, $3.6 million in TRX, $3.4 million in ADA, $2.3 million in BTC, and dozens of other cryptocurrencies.
The most important holding in the assets is the proceeds from the sale of Robinhood stocks, with SBF's Emergent Fidelity Technologies holding $606 million. Other financial assets include:
$119 million in USDT held by Alameda Research at Binance;
$21 million held by Emergent Fidelity Technologies at Marex;
$50 million held by FTX Digital Markets at Moonstone Bank;
$101 million held by FTX Digital Markets at Silvergate.
$7 million held by SBF and another individual at Flagstar Bank
The accounting of seized assets also includes two private jets: a 2009 Bombardier Global 5000 and a 2006 Embraer Legacy.
The court documents also detail more than 250 political donations withdrawn from campaigns and organizations that received them, including amounts donated by other FTX executives allegedly at the direction of SBF. During the last session, one-third of members of Congress accepted donations from SBF or other FTX executives, who also donated to numerous state political organizations. (CoinDesk)

data.title: LBank has launched Based Froc (FROC)
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to official news, LBank has launched Based Froc (FROC) at 12:10 (SGT) on February 19, and opened the FROC/USDT trading pair. Currently, the deposit service is open, and the withdrawal service will be open at 12:00 on February 20.

data.title: South Korea’s Supreme Court rules TerraUSD and Luna are not securities
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News South Korea's Supreme Court ruled that TerraUSD/Luna is not a financial investment product. Although this is not a substantive ruling on the nature of virtual asset securities, it is expected that the ruling will have an impact on lower courts in the future.
The Third Division of the Supreme Court of South Korea rejected the prosecution's appeal against the ruling on the 23rd of last month to reject the request for seizure and preservation of Daniel Shin, former co-CEO of Terraform Labs.
The Supreme Court explained that "taking into account the relevant legal principles and records, the original ruling was reasonable and there was no violation of the Constitution, laws, orders or regulations that affected the trial." Previously, the Southern District Court rejected the prosecution's request to confiscate and preserve Shin's assets in November 2022. The prosecution immediately appealed, but the second trial chamber of the same court also rejected the case in February 2023.
In particular, the second instance court ruled in its rejection decision that "it is difficult to regard the virtual currency Luna as a 'financial investment product' regulated by the Capital Markets and Financial Investment Business Act based solely on the materials submitted by the prosecution." (Etoday)

data.title: AscendEX will launch Based Froc (FROC)
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News AscendEX (formerly BitMax) will launch Based Froc (FROC) at 13:00 on February 19, opening the FROC/USDT trading pair, and recharge is now open.
The Meme Research Institute team under the AscendEX platform screened out high-quality Memes for listing based on on-chain data and project background, and the platform's Memecoin initial launch rate was as high as 80%.

data.title: CZ: All funds received in the donation wallet will be donated, possibly to those who lost money on TST or Broccolis
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News CZ wrote on X: "There is an interesting situation in the field of cryptocurrency: When you try to make money quickly, you often lose money. When you donate money, you get more. I donated 150 BNB (about 100,000 US dollars) to a college student, and he took out 50,000 US dollars to help Libra victims. (The student seemed to make a lot of money on BNB.) Now this address I made public has received more BNB than I donated. There are more tokens. I won't keep a satoshi. It may be donated, most likely to those who lost money on TST or some Broccolis. This is not an endorsement of any token. Don't over-interpret this."

data.title: Brevan Howard CEO: The crypto industry has turned a page after the FTX collapse, but risk management is still needed around the clock
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News Since the FTX crash in 2023 destroyed billions of dollars of investor wealth, the crypto ecosystem has made great progress. However, TradFi experts said at the Hong Kong Consensus event on Wednesday that the entire industry needs to work harder to become indestructible.
Gautam Sharma, CEO and chief investment officer of Brevan Howard, said: "Now there are traditional players entering the crypto field, especially for us, most of our transactions are settled through exchanges, you actually put your assets with the custodian, and you can trade on the exchange at the same time. So in the 18 months since that (FTX crash), the technology has made great progress, but there is more work to do."
Sharma emphasized the need for round-the-clock risk management, including market risk, counterparty risk and credit risk.
Mike Kuehnel, CEO of market maker Flow Traders, stressed the need to make innovation transparent to gain investor confidence and ensure “data availability and liquidity flows without fragmentation,” adding: “Getting the best price and enabling users to trade at any time are key factors.” (CoinDesk)

data.title: Walrus: Airdrop NFT has been issued, corresponding to 4% of the total supply of 5 billion WAL tokens
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News Decentralized storage and data availability protocol Walrus announced on X that user wallets eligible for the Walrus User Drop have received the Walrus Airdrop NFT, allowing them to claim a certain number of WAL tokens, which can be redeemed through claim.walrus.xyz when the Walrus Mainnet is launched. This initial airdrop accounts for 4% of the 5 billion WAL token supply, and another 6% is reserved for future community allocations. The airdrop is directly allocated to community members to reward early participants who participate in the Walrus and Sui ecosystems in a meaningful way.
In addition, the official reminder is that the NFT is soul-bound, which means it cannot be traded or transferred to another wallet. Users need to make sure that their wallets are updated to the latest version.

data.title: Binance will remove multiple spot trading pairs and terminate related trading robot services
data.description: According to the official announcement, Binance will remove and stop trading the following spot trading pairs at 03:00 (UTC) on February 21, 2025: DOGS/BRL, FET/BRL, NEIRO/BRL, NEIRO/EUR, NOT/EUR. At the same time, Binance will terminate the trading robot service for the above spot trading pairs at the same time. Users need to update or cancel their spot trading robots before the service is terminated to avoid potential losses. (This newsletter is generated by AI)

data.title: Coinbase and its executives are sued by shareholders, alleging they misled investors about bankruptcy risks and violated securities laws
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News Coinbase shareholder Wenduo Guo filed a lawsuit in the New Jersey Federal Court on February 18, accusing Coinbase and its leadership of failing to disclose that customer assets may be considered part of Coinbase's bankruptcy property, making retail customers unsecured creditors, thereby misleading investors about bankruptcy risks and violating securities laws.
The lawsuit claims that before Coinbase went public in April 2021, at least 75 cryptocurrency exchanges collapsed, leaving these customers unable to recover their digital assets, "despite repeated statements from the company's management to the contrary, Coinbase is no different in terms of the risk of losing digital assets in bankruptcy."
The complaint also claims that Coinbase failed to disclose that it was engaging in proprietary trading to make up for the decline in cryptocurrency prices, which is a "dangerous practice of using company funds to trade assets." In addition, executives including CEO Brian Armstrong (named in the lawsuit) sold large quantities of stock and made hundreds of millions of dollars in personal profits from it. (Cointelegraph)
